● Shader Files · Compute · TGSM · Platform Guards

.usf / .ush Shader Reference

Engine: UE 5.x
Context: .usf · .ush · Compute Shaders
Target: SM5 / SM6 / ES3.1
⚗ HLSL Reference
.usf / .ush File Rules
File Structure Basics
✓Use #pragma once or #ifndef/#define include guards
✓Include /Engine/Public/Platform.ush as the base header
✓Declare module macro: YOURMODULE_API
✓Use virtual shader path mapping for plugin custom paths
✓.ush = header (shared types/helpers) · .usf = source (entry points)
Include Guard Pattern
// Option A: pragma once
#pragma once

// Option B: traditional guards
#ifndef MY_SHADER_COMMON_USH
#define MY_SHADER_COMMON_USH

#include "/Engine/Public/Platform.ush"
#include "/MyPlugin/Private/MyCommon.ush"

// Shared types and helpers here
struct FMyData {
  float3 Position;
  float  Radius;
  float  Intensity;
  uint   Flags;
};

#endif // MY_SHADER_COMMON_USH
Platform & Feature Guards
// Feature level guard
#if FEATURE_LEVEL >= FEATURE_LEVEL_SM5
  // SM5+ only code (compute, UAV, etc.)
#endif

// SM6-only features
#if FEATURE_LEVEL >= FEATURE_LEVEL_SM6
  // WaveOps, RayQuery, etc.
#endif

// Platform-specific
#if COMPILER_HLSL
  // HLSL-specific path
#elif COMPILER_GLSL || COMPILER_GLSL_ES3_1
  // Mobile / OpenGL path
#endif

// Vulkan-specific
#if VULKAN_PROFILE
  // Vulkan workarounds
#endif
Compute Shader Patterns
Compute Shader Entry Point
#include "/Engine/Public/Platform.ush"
#include "/MyPlugin/Private/MyCommon.ush"

// Thread group sizing
#define NUM_THREADS_X 8
#define NUM_THREADS_Y 8

// Resource bindings
Texture2D<float4>   InputTexture;
RWTexture2D<float4> OutputTexture;
uint Resolution;

[numthreads(NUM_THREADS_X, NUM_THREADS_Y, 1)]
void MainCS(
    uint3 DispatchThreadID : SV_DispatchThreadID,
    uint3 GroupThreadID   : SV_GroupThreadID,
    uint3 GroupID         : SV_GroupID)
{
  uint x = DispatchThreadID.x;
  uint y = DispatchThreadID.y;
  if (x >= Resolution || y >= Resolution)
    return;

  // Read, process, write
  float4 Val = InputTexture.Load(int3(x, y, 0));
  OutputTexture[uint2(x, y)] = Val;
}
Compute · Common Mistakes
✗Missing bounds check — always guard: if (x >= Res || y >= Res) return;
✗Wrong dispatch size — dispatch = ceil(Resolution / ThreadGroupSize)
✗UAV format mismatch — RWTexture format must match what C++ creates
✗Missing barriers — always GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync() after TGSM writes
✗No ddx/ddy in compute — these are pixel shader only
!Thread group size — 8×8 is common; 16×16 may exceed shared memory limits
Resource Types
Texture2DRead-only texture (SRV)
RWTexture2DRead-write texture (UAV)
BufferRead-only typed buffer (SRV)
RWBufferRead-write typed buffer (UAV)
StructuredBufferRead-only structured buffer
RWStructuredBufferRead-write structured buffer
SamplerStateTexture sampler (bilinear, point, etc.)
Thread Group Shared Memory (TGSM)
TGSM Pattern

Use groupshared memory to share data between threads in a group, reducing global memory reads:

// Declare tile cache
#define TILE_DIM 10  // 8 + 1-pixel halo each side
groupshared float TileCache[TILE_DIM][TILE_DIM];

// 1. Load tile from global memory
int2 LoadPos = GroupID.xy * 8
  + GroupThreadID.xy - 1;  // halo offset
LoadPos = clamp(LoadPos,
  int2(0,0), int2(Res-1,Res-1));
TileCache[GroupThreadID.y][GroupThreadID.x]
  = SourceGrid.Load(int3(LoadPos, 0)).r;

// 2. Sync all threads
GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync();

// 3. Read from cache (fast)
float Center = TileCache[ly][lx];
float Left   = TileCache[ly][lx-1];
float Right  = TileCache[ly][lx+1];
TGSM Rules
✓groupshared keyword declares shared memory — visible to all threads in group
✓Always sync with GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync() between write and read
✓Halo loading — load 1+ pixel border around tile for stencil operations
✓Memory budget — 32KB per thread group on most GPUs; float[10][10] = 400 bytes
!Bank conflicts — stride of 32 floats causes conflicts; pad arrays if needed
!Boundary clamping — halo loads must clamp to grid bounds to avoid OOB reads
TGSM Macro Pattern

Use macros to make TGSM reusable across passes:

// Declare macro for float tiles
#define DECLARE_TILE_CACHE_FLOAT(Name) \
  groupshared float Name[TILE_DIM][TILE_DIM]

// Load macro with scroll compensation
#define LOAD_TILE_SCROLLED(Cache, Tex, ...) \
  { /* load with offset + clamp */ \
    GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync(); }

// Usage in compute shader
DECLARE_TILE_CACHE_FLOAT(SDFCache);
LOAD_TILE_SCROLLED(SDFCache, SDFTex, ...);
Quick Reference
Feature Level Capabilities
FeatureES3.1SM5SM6
Wave Intrinsics✗✗✓
RayQuery / DXR✗✗✓
ddx / ddy✗✓✓
Bindless Resources✗✗✓
half precision✓✓✓
Compute / UAV✗✓✓
Geometry Shaders✗✓✓
TGSM (groupshared)✗✓✓
Typed SRV Buffers✗✓✓
UE Type Keywords & Hints
MaterialFloatAdapts precision per platform automatically
half / half316-bit — use on mobile for performance
float / float332-bit — default for PC / console
BRANCH[branch] hint — forces dynamic branching
FLATTEN[flatten] hint — forces both paths evaluated
UNROLL[unroll] — unroll loop at compile time
LOOP[loop] — force dynamic loop (required for dynamic bounds)
Common Shader Errors
✗Matrix multiply order — HLSL is row-vector × matrix; GLSL is column-major
✗Missing include guard — double-include causes redefinition errors
✗Virtual path mismatch — /MyPlugin/ must match AddShaderSourceDirectoryMapping
✗Uninitialized groupshared — TGSM is undefined until written
✗Integer division — 1/2 = 0; cast to float explicitly: 1.0/2.0
!Scalar broadcast — float3(1) is valid HLSL but prefer float3(1,1,1) for clarity; some older compiler versions warn
Texture Sampling & SDF Patterns
Sampling Methods
// Load (integer coords, no filtering)
float4 Val = Tex.Load(int3(x, y, 0));

// SampleLevel (UV coords, explicit mip)
float4 Val = Tex.SampleLevel(Sampler, UV, 0);

// In Material Custom Node:
// Texture2DSample(Tex, TexSampler, UV)
// Texture2DSampleLevel(Tex, TexSampler, UV, Lod)

// External texture (registered via GUID)
int32 TexCode = Compiler->ExternalTexture(GUID);
int32 Sample  = Compiler->TextureSample(
  TexCode, UVCode, SAMPLERTYPE_External);
Central-Difference Gradient

Compute gradient from a scalar field (e.g. SDF distance):

// Read neighbors
float Left  = Grid.Load(int3(max(x-1, 0),      y, 0)).r;
float Right = Grid.Load(int3(min(x+1, Res-1),  y, 0)).r;
float Up    = Grid.Load(int3(x, max(y-1, 0),      0)).r;
float Down  = Grid.Load(int3(x, min(y+1, Res-1),  0)).r;

// Central difference
float2 Gradient = float2(Right - Left, Down - Up);
float  Len = length(Gradient);
if (Len > 0.001)
  Gradient /= Len;  // Normalize

// Output: R=dist, GB=direction, A=mag
OutputRT[Pixel] = float4(
  NormDist, Gradient.x, Gradient.y, Len);
SDF Math Helpers
// Smooth minimum (polynomial blend)
float SmoothMin(float a, float b, float k)
{
  if (k <= 0.0) return min(a, b);
  float h = saturate(0.5 + 0.5 * (b - a) / k);
  return lerp(b, a, h) - k * h * (1.0 - h);
}

// Distance to point
float PointDist(float2 P, float2 C, float R)
{
  return length(P - C) - R;
}

// Distance to line segment
float SegmentDist(float2 P,
    float2 A, float2 B, float R)
{
  float2 AB = B - A;
  float  t  = saturate(dot(P - A, AB) / dot(AB, AB));
  return length(P - A - AB * t) - R;
}
⚡ In-Engine Optimization
Compute Shader Performance Rules
Occupancy & ALU
✓Target 75%+ GPU occupancy — reduce register pressure by reusing temporaries and simplifying arithmetic chains
✓Thread group size 8×8 (64 threads) — safe on all targets including mobile ES3.1 and SM5 desktop
!16×16 (256 threads) — risks exceeding 32KB shared memory limit on some mobile/integrated GPUs; test explicitly
✓Minimize register use — declare variables inside loop bodies where possible; fewer live registers = higher occupancy
!Avoid branch divergence — if half the threads take each side of an if, you pay 2× the cost; restructure with lerp / select
Memory Bandwidth
✓TGSM is the primary bandwidth tool — load a tile of data once into groupshared, read it many times within the group
✓Coalesce global reads/writes — adjacent threads should access adjacent memory addresses (stride-1 access patterns)
✗Scattered UAV writes are slow — random-address writes break GPU memory coalescing; prefer sorted or sequential indices
✗Never read/write the same UAV in one dispatch — use ping-pong buffers (read A, write B; next frame swap)
✓Minimize RW texture round-trips — avoid dispatches that write a texture and immediately read it in the same pass
Thread Group Size Cheat Sheet
SizeThreadsTGSM BudgetRecommended For
8×8×164~1.6KB floatSafe on all targets — textures, SDF, general 2D
8×8×164~3.2KB float2Gradient / neighbor reads with halo
16×16×1256~6.4KB floatPC/SM5+ only — larger tiles, reduce dispatch overhead
64×1×164—1D passes: histograms, prefix sums, buffer ops
32×1×132—Mobile-safe 1D — matches warp/wavefront size
🔬 Engine Internals
Dispatch & Resource Rules
Dispatch Setup Rules
Dispatch sizeceil(Resolution / ThreadGroupSize) — never dispatch without a bounds check inside the shader; out-of-bounds threads must return early
Ping-pong buffersNever read and write the same UAV in a single dispatch — use two separate buffers (A and B) and swap which one is read vs written each frame
Async computeOverlap GPU work (e.g. shadow depth pass + simulation) using the async compute queue — reduces overall frame latency without extra CPU cost
RDG resourcesUse FRDGBuilder to declare textures and buffers — the Render Dependency Graph handles barrier insertion and lifetime automatically
Indirect dispatchUse when dispatch dimensions are determined on the GPU (e.g. particle counts) — avoids an expensive CPU readback stall
RDG (Render Dependency Graph) Patterns

UE5 native render pass system — always prefer RDG over manual render commands:

// Declare RDG texture
FRDGTextureRef OutputTex = GraphBuilder.CreateTexture(
  FRDGTextureDesc::Create2D(
    FIntPoint(Width, Height),
    PF_FloatRGBA,
    FClearValueBinding::Black,
    TexCreate_UAV | TexCreate_ShaderResource),
  TEXT("MyOutputTexture"));

// Pass parameters
FMyShaderParameters* PassParams =
  GraphBuilder.AllocParameters<FMyShaderParameters>();
PassParams->OutputTexture =
  GraphBuilder.CreateUAV(OutputTex);

// Add compute pass
GraphBuilder.AddPass(
  RDG_EVENT_NAME("MyComputePass"),
  PassParams,
  ERDGPassFlags::Compute,
  [PassParams, Width, Height](FRHIComputeCommandList& RHICmdList)
  {
    // Dispatch here
  });
Profiling Compute Shaders
stat gpuConsole command — shows GPU time per pass; look for your pass name from RDG_EVENT_NAME
RenderDocFrame capture tool — inspect every dispatch, view UAV contents, check thread occupancy
GPU Insights (5.4+)Built-in Unreal Insights GPU view — shows compute pass durations on a timeline
r.RDG.Debug 1Enables RDG validation — catches missing barriers, resource aliasing, and UAV hazards
profilegpuConsole command — dumps per-pass GPU timings to the output log
Platform-Specific Performance Notes
Filter:
SM5 / SM6 Desktop
✓Wave intrinsics (SM6) — WaveActiveSum, WaveActiveBallot for prefix sums and culling; no atomic lock needed
✓Bindless resources (SM6) — large descriptor heaps; avoid per-draw binding changes on DX12 / Vulkan
✓16×16 thread groups safe — desktop GPUs have ≥32KB shared memory and high register files
!RayQuery (SM6) — only available in compute/pixel shaders with DXR-enabled hardware and feature level SM6
Mobile (ES3.1)
✓Use half precision — mobile GPUs process 16-bit at 2× the throughput of 32-bit
✗No compute / UAV on ES3.1 — compute shaders require at minimum Feature Level SM5; mobile = vertex + pixel only
✗No ddx / ddy — derivatives are unavailable in ES3.1 pixel shaders too; approximate with explicit UV derivatives
!16 sampler cap — total texture samples (all stages) must stay under 16 on most mobile GPUs
!Test with Mobile Preview — Editor viewport mode that simulates ES3.1 constraints without a device
Vulkan / Metal Specifics
✓VULKAN_PROFILE guard — use for Vulkan-specific workarounds (different NDC, subpass load, etc.)
!Explicit memory barriers on Vulkan — RDG handles these automatically; avoid raw RHICmdList transitions
!Metal tile shaders — available on Apple Silicon; no direct HLSL equivalent; use UE5 RHI abstraction
✓Vulkan subpass inputs — allows reading previous render pass output without a full texture read; great for deferred passes
